mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 during periods of rising FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during post-recession recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes correlate with positive 20d forward returns, or the out-of-sample R-squared of the inflation-return relationship is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand and supports higher equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal cumulative alpha turns negative within 12 months of activation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate positive 20d returns with a win rate above 55%, or the interaction term in a predictive model is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy operates above potential output, a real federal funds rate exceeding 1.5% signals restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when the real rate exceeds 1.5% during above-potential expansions, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45% over a 3-year period.
